issues.description: In 16:9 aspect ratio mode, the map becomes a very thin horizontal strip (approx 220px tall on 390px wide screen) with large dark/black letterbox areas above and below. Nearly 60% of the map viewport is wasted dark space, making the map content barely usable.
issues.expected: On portrait mobile, 16:9 ratio should either auto-switch to a more suitable layout, warn the user, or scale the map to be more usable.
issues.actual: Map content compressed to a thin strip. Dark letterbox dominates the viewport in 16:9 mode on a portrait phone.
issues.element: Map container in 16:9 aspect ratio mode

issues.description: The 'Collapse route panel' button does not produce a visible change to the bottom sheet on mobile. Tapping it does not minimize the route list or give more map visibility. The button remains in 'expanded' state.
issues.expected: Collapse should minimize the bottom sheet to a summary bar, revealing more of the map behind it.
issues.actual: Button click produces no visible layout change. The bottom sheet remains at the same height and position.
issues.element: Collapse route panel button (bottom sheet header)

issues.description: Search results dropdown items are not represented in the accessibility tree. The listbox 'City search results' appears empty in the a11y snapshot even though 5+ results are visually rendered and tappable.
issues.expected: Search result items should have role='option' within the listbox for screen reader compatibility.
issues.actual: The listbox element shows no child option elements in the accessibility tree despite visual results for 'Paris' query being displayed.

issues.description: The 'More' aspect ratio button (28px tall) is below the 44px minimum touch target height. All other aspect ratio buttons (16:9, 9:16, 1:1) meet the 44px height but 'More' is only 68x28.
issues.expected: All interactive elements should have minimum 44x44pt touch targets per Apple HIG.
issues.actual: 'More' button measures 68x28, with only 28px height -- well below the 44px minimum.
issues.element: More button in aspect ratio toolbar row

issues.description: An unlabeled icon button (32x32) exists between the project name and share button in the top toolbar. It has no aria-label, title, or text content, making it completely inaccessible to screen readers and unclear in purpose.
issues.expected: All buttons must have an accessible label. This appears to be an 'open in new tab' or 'redirect' icon and should be labeled accordingly.
issues.actual: Button has no aria-label, no title, no text content. Renders as an SVG arrow icon at 32x32 (also below 44px touch target minimum).
issues.element: Unlabeled icon button in TopToolbar (between project name and share)

issues.description: The expand chevron buttons in route stop cards are only 24x24 pixels -- well below the 44px minimum touch target. These are important interactive elements for expanding stop details.
issues.expected: Expand chevrons should have at least 44x44 touch target areas (can use padding/hit area expansion).
issues.actual: Expand button measures 24x24, making it difficult to tap accurately on mobile.
issues.element: Expand chevron buttons on route stop cards

issues.description: City name buttons in route stop cards (e.g., 'Seattle', 'Tokyo') have touch targets of only 43x22 -- the height of 22px is extremely small for a tappable element labeled 'Tap to edit stop name'.
issues.expected: City name buttons should have at least 44px tall touch targets since they are primary interactive elements.
issues.actual: City name button measures 43x22, making it very hard to tap accurately.
issues.element: City name buttons in route stop list

issues.description: The Projects dialog (project switcher) shows the playback controls bar bleeding through behind the dialog overlay. The playback bar's z-index appears higher than the dialog backdrop, creating a visual layering issue.
issues.expected: Dialog backdrop should fully cover all underlying UI elements including the playback bar.
issues.actual: Playback controls bar (play button, slider, time labels) is visible through/over the projects dialog backdrop.
issues.element: Projects dialog vs playback controls z-index

resolved_from_round_2.description: Time labels now visible during playback -- timestamps (e.g., '0:15 / 0:52') display at full opacity during active playback. Confirmed visible in playback screenshots at multiple points in timeline.
Share button enlarged from 32x32 to 44x44. Aspect ratio buttons (16:9, 9:16, 1:1) enlarged from ~48x30 to 48x44. These now meet the 44px minimum touch target guideline.

Project name truncation improved from ~5-6 characters ('Paci...') to ~17 characters ('Pacific Rim Adven...'). The name is now recognizable at a glance. max-width was increased as noted in the Round 2 fixes.

Quick controls dialog (from 'More' button) did not open during this test -- the button appeared non-responsive. May be a testing artifact; marking as unable to verify but the reported truncation issue from R2 may still exist.

resolved_from_round_1: No horizontal overflow on any screen (scrollWidth === clientWidth === 390)
Map uses free aspect ratio by default on mobile -- fills available viewport
Map fitBounds shows all 7 route markers on initial load
Playback controls visible and functional during playback
Toolbar contained in single row without overflow
'TraceRecap /' prefix hidden on mobile as intended
Map centered vertically in constrained viewport

initial_view.notes: Toolbar visible and readable, project name recognizable, aspect ratio buttons visible. Map area is dark/zoomed out but functional. Bottom sheet with route list properly positioned. No overflow. Deductions: map area feels small behind the expanded bottom sheet, one unlabeled button.

map_rendering.notes: Map renders properly in free and 9:16 modes. All markers visible. Route lines shown during playback. Photo overlays display correctly during ARRIVE phases. Deductions: 16:9 mode renders an unusably thin map strip on portrait mobile, large dark letterbox areas.

playback_experience.notes: Major improvement. Playback goes full-screen immersive. Route labels visible (city names with emoji). Time labels now showing during playback at full opacity. Pause/play controls responsive. Photo polaroid overlays display at proper size during ARRIVE phase. Waveform visualization in slider is a nice touch.

bottom_sheet.notes: Bottom sheet renders and scrolls through route stops properly. Scrollable content (1672px scroll height in 623px container) works. Deductions: Collapse button has no visible effect, sheet cannot be dragged to different snap points, sheet obscures significant map area.

menu.notes: Menu renders as proper mobile bottom sheet pattern. All items visible: Undo, Redo, Import Route, Save Route, Settings, Clear Route. Each item is 358x44 -- full width with proper touch target height. Close button visible. Clear Route appropriately highlighted in red.

export_dialog.notes: Export dialog is well-designed for mobile. Resolution cards (103x92) clearly displayed. Export Video button (326x48) is full-width and prominent. Estimated size shown. Settings section readable. Close button properly sized (44x44). No overflow or clipping.

touch_targets.notes: Significant improvement from R2 on toolbar buttons (share, aspect ratio now 44px). However, several secondary elements still undersized: 'More' button (28px tall), expand chevrons (24x24), city name buttons (43x22), unlabeled icon button (32x32). Map controls (zoom +/-) are standard Mapbox at 32x32.

search.notes: Search works correctly. Typing 'Paris' shows 5 relevant results with location details. Dropdown properly positioned within viewport. Deductions: search results not in accessibility tree (ARIA issue), no loading indicator visible during search.

overall.notes: Solid improvement from Round 1 (3/10) and Round 2 (7/10). Key Round 2 fixes confirmed: time labels visible during playback, touch targets enlarged on share/aspect ratio buttons, project name more readable. Playback experience is now genuinely good (8/10). Export and menu dialogs are polished (9/10). The remaining issues are mostly medium-severity: broken collapse panel, 16:9 letterbox on portrait, accessibility gaps (unlabeled button, missing ARIA on search), and small touch targets on secondary elements. The app is usable on mobile but not yet delightful -- still feels like a desktop-first experience adapted for mobile rather than a mobile-native experience.

summary: Round 3 confirms that all targeted Round 2 fixes were successfully applied: time labels are visible during playback, share and aspect ratio buttons meet 44px minimum, and the project name is now recognizable. Playback is the standout improvement -- immersive mode with visible controls, route labels, and properly sized photo overlays scores 8/10. Export dialog (9/10) and menu (9/10) are genuinely well-done mobile experiences. However, the score remains at 7/10 overall because several medium-severity issues persist (non-functional collapse button, search ARIA gaps, 16:9 letterbox) and new issues were identified (unlabeled button, undersized expand chevrons and city name buttons, z-index bleed on projects dialog). To reach 8+, the app needs: (1) functional bottom sheet collapse/snap points, (2) accessible labels on all buttons, (3) minimum 44px touch targets on all interactive elements, and (4) a solution for landscape ratios on portrait mobile.
path_to_8_plus: Fix the collapse route panel button to actually minimize the bottom sheet, giving users more map area
Add aria-label to the unlabeled icon button in the toolbar and increase its touch target to 44x44
Increase touch targets on expand chevrons (24px -> 44px) and city name buttons (22px -> 44px) using padding or hit area expansion
Fix search results ARIA -- add role='option' to listbox items
Fix 'More' button height from 28px to 44px minimum
Address z-index layering so dialogs fully cover the playback bar
Consider hiding 16:9 option on portrait mobile or adding a 'best for landscape' label
